lucidBlocksBeginnerGuide.title: Gamble With Your Friends Steam Download and Price
lucidBlocksBeginnerGuide.intro: Check the base game, soundtrack DLC, and current Steam discounts before you buy. A quick pricing pass prevents duplicate purchases across bundles.

steps.title: Check the Base Game Listing
steps.description: Confirm the current base-game price on the official Steam page first so your team starts from the same purchase baseline.

steps.title: Review the Soundtrack DLC Separately
steps.description: The soundtrack is sold as separate DLC, so decide whether your group needs it before checkout to avoid unnecessary add-ons.

steps.title: Compare Bundle Value Before Buying
steps.description: If you want both game and soundtrack, compare bundle pricing against standalone purchases and include active launch discounts.

steps.title: Check Cross-Game Bundle Contents
steps.description: Some bundles include other TENSTACK titles. Confirm whether your group wants only this game or a wider catalog package.

steps.title: Track Price History for Better Timing
steps.description: Use SteamDB trend data to see recent lows and decide whether to buy now or wait for the next sale cycle.

quickTips: Steam launch discounts can end quickly, so verify dates before sharing buy advice.
Bundle pages often update after launch; refresh before final checkout.
For team play, prioritize everyone owning the base game over optional DLC.
Bookmark both Steam Store and SteamDB pages to track future discount windows.

lucidBlocksApotheosisCrafting.title: Gamble With Your Friends Gameplay Overview
lucidBlocksApotheosisCrafting.intro: Gamble With Your Friends is a co-op casino crawler where one shared account and one shared debt shape every decision from floor to floor.

cards.name: 1-6 Player Online Co-op
cards.description: Runs support solo or full party play, but the core loop is built around team calls under quota pressure.

cards.name: Shared Bankroll Economy
cards.description: Every bet draws from one account, so reckless all-ins hurt the entire team instead of a single player.

cards.name: 5-Minute Quota Days
cards.description: Each day is short and tense: reach quota before time expires, or the run falls behind immediately.

cards.name: Floor Progression and Endings
cards.description: The run climbs themed floors and branches into multiple outcomes based on risk control and team execution.

lucidBlocksToolsAndWeapons.title: Gamble With Your Friends Beginner Guide
lucidBlocksToolsAndWeapons.intro: Start with a simple team plan and avoid early volatility. Most failed first runs come from uncoordinated betting, not bad luck.

items.name: Lobby Plan Before Queue
items.description: Assign one caller for bankroll updates, one timer watcher, and one item lead before entering the first day.

items.name: Pick One Item Goal
items.description: Do not scatter tickets across random buys. Choose one item path that supports your first two floors.

items.name: Start on Familiar Tables
items.description: Use stable opening tables your group understands so everyone can synchronize bet size and timing.

items.name: Use Small Bet Calibration
items.description: Open with small stakes to test rhythm, then raise only after the team agrees on target bankroll pace.

items.name: Call Every Large Bet Out Loud
items.description: Major wagers should always be announced first to prevent overlapping all-ins from separate players.

items.name: Protect Quota-Ready Bankroll
items.description: Once quota is in range, shift to preservation mode and avoid unnecessary high-volatility tables.

items.name: Use Swing Plays Only on Pressure
items.description: Commit to high-risk recovery lines only when timer pressure is real and backup options are exhausted.

items.name: Debrief Each Day Quickly
items.description: Review which table choices worked, then adjust item timing and risk limits before the next floor.

lucidBlocksStorageAndInventory.title: Gamble With Your Friends Co-op Multiplayer
lucidBlocksStorageAndInventory.intro: Multiplayer is friends-first: lobby setup quality matters as much as in-run decisions. Use consistent host and privacy settings for smoother sessions.

solutions.name: Friends-Only or Invite-Only Lobby
solutions.description: Pick explicit privacy mode before invites so random joins do not interrupt role setup and item planning.

solutions.name: Host on Strongest Connection
solutions.description: The most stable host should create the lobby to reduce desync and reconnection issues during high-pressure rounds.

solutions.name: Invite Through Steam Friends
solutions.description: Use the Steam friend list for fast joining, then verify everyone loads in before the first quota day begins.

solutions.name: Set Voice and Callout Rules
solutions.description: Define short callouts for bet size, timer checkpoints, and emergency swing plays to keep comms clean.

solutions.name: Use Discord for Group Assembly
solutions.description: Official community channels help fill parties quickly when your Steam friend list is short.

solutions.name: Re-host After Settings Changes
solutions.description: If privacy or invite state behaves inconsistently, re-host once to apply settings cleanly.

managementTips: No public matchmaking means teammate quality directly determines run stability.
Keep lobby privacy strict when practicing high-risk strategy routes.
One stable host across sessions improves reconnect consistency.
Use the same callout vocabulary every run to reduce chaos under timer pressure.

lucidBlocksWorldRegions.title: Gamble With Your Friends Quota Strategy
lucidBlocksWorldRegions.intro: Every casino day lasts 5 minutes and every player spends from one shared bank. Clear quota first, then choose how much risk your team can afford.

steps.title: Set the shared-bank rule first
steps.body: One bank account and one debt means every large bet affects the whole team. Decide in lobby who can call big wagers and who tracks timer pressure.
steps.action: Use one caller for large bets, one timer watcher, and one item carrier.
steps.avoid: Do not let every player free-bet at the same time.

steps.title: Treat the 5-minute day like a sprint
steps.body: The day timer is short, so table-hopping without a reason burns the most valuable resource. Choose a starting table before the run begins.
steps.action: Pick the opening table in advance and move only with team agreement.
steps.avoid: Do not spend the first minute debating where to play.

steps.title: Secure quota before chasing highlights
steps.body: The safest pattern is to clear quota, then decide whether to push achievements, higher floors, or bigger multipliers.
steps.action: Pause once you are above quota and align on the next risk.
steps.avoid: Do not chase personal comeback plays with shared money.

steps.title: Use simple games for early bankroll control
steps.body: Floor 1 games like Roulette, Blackjack, Duck Race, Slots, Street Craps, and Wheel of Fortune are strong training tables for team rhythm.
steps.action: Let one player lead complex rules while the team protects bankroll pace.
steps.avoid: Do not max bet games your team has not learned.

steps.title: Spend Tickets on run-changing items
steps.body: Tickets are strongest when tied to a table plan. Decide who carries each item and exactly when it should be used.
steps.action: Before buying, confirm owner, target table, and trigger window.
steps.avoid: Do not buy random items without a game plan.

steps.title: Push higher floors only with a cushion
steps.body: The casino has four themed floors, and later floors introduce bigger swings and harsher punishment for mistakes.
steps.action: Bring extra money, item support, and a clear objective before pushing Floor 3 or Floor 4.
steps.avoid: Do not improvise on late floors with the entire shared bank.

steps.title: Stop after the team wins the day
steps.body: Stopping is part of winning. If quota is secure and timer is low, preserving the bank is usually stronger than forcing one more high-risk bet.
steps.action: Call the stop point before opening another table.
steps.avoid: Do not treat one lucky win as a reason to double risk again.

lucidBlocksCreaturesAndEnemies.title: Gamble With Your Friends Floors Guide
lucidBlocksCreaturesAndEnemies.intro: The casino tower has four themed floors with randomized table layouts. This floor map combines game pools, achievement targets, and practical team focus for each stage.

floors.role: Learning floor and early bankroll control
achievementGoals: All on Red: Bet a lot of money on Roulette.
YARLL!!!: Bet on YARL and win on Duck Race.
Spin The Wheel: Win 3 high-stake Wheel of Fortune games.
Lucky 7: Get 7 on your first Street Craps roll.
Risky Move: Win Blackjack with a hand below 10.
No Bust Run: Win 4 Blackjack games in a row.
Jackpot Hunter: Win with 10X on Slot Machine.
floors.teamPlan: Use Floor 1 to lock communication rhythm, learn tables, and establish stop-loss rules before larger bets.

achievementGoals: Winner Winner Penguin Dinner: Win at least 3X twice in a row on Penguin Cross.
Spot On: Bet a lot of money on Keno.
Greed Test: Win at least 10X on Crash.
Roll the Odds: Profit a lot of money on HiLo.
Clean Fall: Win 24X on Plinko.
Spin Streak: Win 3 high-stake Money Wheel games in a row.
floors.teamPlan: Move here with item support and a fixed plan before touching high-variance tables like Crash, Plinko, or Money Wheel.

achievementGoals: Traffic Monster: Win at least 4X on Penguin Cross.
Keep It Spinning: Spend a lot of money on Wheel of Fortune.
Natural Roller: Win Street Craps during point phase.
Step Into Fire: Lose 4 Dragon Tower games in a row.
Poker Face: Win 1P Poker without locking cards, with Two of a Kind or better.
Perfect Sweep: Win at least 20X on Mine Sweeper.
No Safe Rolls: Win 10X on HiLo.
floors.teamPlan: Pick one main objective per run and avoid jumping between unfamiliar high-pressure tables.

floors.role: Late-run push and rare achievement floor
achievementGoals: Ball in Motion: Spend a lot of money on Roulette.
No Hesitation: Spend a lot of money on Penguin Cross.
Marked to Win: Profit a lot of money on Keno.
Straight Down: Lose 7 Plinko games in a row.
House Breaker: Win a lot of money on Blackjack.
Safe Exit: Win Crash 4 times in a row.
Flameproof: Win at least 3X three times in a row on Dragon Tower.
Unstoppable Quack: Win Duck Race 2 times in a row.
No Detonations: Win 5X three times in a row on Mine Sweeper.
High Risk Wedge: Lose Money Wheel 5 games in a row.
floors.teamPlan: Use Floor 4 for planned late-run pushes only after building bankroll cushion and securing item backup.

lucidBlocksMobilityGear.title: Gamble With Your Friends Games Of Chance
lucidBlocksMobilityGear.intro: Steam highlights 17 chance games. This list focuses on the named games most tied to floor progression, achievements, and team strategy planning.

items.howToUse: Use for simple coordinated pushes when the team agrees on entry size and stop point.
Easy to read, but rapid repeat bets can drain shared funds without strict cap rules.
Best with one caller leading hit-or-stand decisions to avoid conflicting team calls.
Use smaller stake windows or item support when chasing the 10X target.
Assign one roller and one caller so the team keeps pace and avoids rushed mistakes.
Useful for recovery attempts and specific goals, but risky as a default quota route.
Learn it in Floor 2 before committing serious late-floor bankroll.
Treat as a planned objective, not a blind first-table decision.
Define cash-out targets before each round because greed pressure is built into this game.
Strong for disciplined players who can stop on target instead of forcing one more call.
Fast and high-variance, so commit only money the team is prepared to risk.
Needs a strict stop point and full team alignment before repeated attempts.
Treat as a specialist late-floor game rather than a casual quota option.
Review the no-lock condition before committing large bets on the Poker Face route.
Use for dedicated multiplier pushes once bankroll cushion and table understanding are ready.

lucidBlocksFarmingAndGrowth.title: Gamble With Your Friends Best Tables Strategy
lucidBlocksFarmingAndGrowth.intro: Choose tables by timer pressure, team discipline, item loadout, and how clearly your group understands the win condition. The best table is not always the highest-multiplier table.

items.label: Best Starter Tables
items.floorClues: Roulette appears in Floor 1 and Floor 4 achievement clues. Blackjack appears in Floor 1 and Floor 4 achievement clues.
items.bestFor: Fast quota pushes and controlled decision-making.
items.strategy: Use one shot-caller and one main bettor. Roulette is best for simple planned swings, while Blackjack is best when one player understands hit, stand, and when to stop.
items.avoidWhen: Avoid this when the team is chasing losses or multiple players are placing conflicting shared-bank bets.

items.label: Strong With Coordination
items.floorClues: Street Craps appears in Floor 1 and Floor 3 achievement clues. Slot Machine appears in Floor 1 achievement clues.
items.bestFor: Item-supported runs and teams that can follow a clear table role.
items.strategy: For Street Craps, assign one roller and one caller, read the target before touching dice, and stop after the planned payout. For Slot Machine, use small bets or protective item windows instead of blind spam.
items.avoidWhen: Avoid this when players crowd the dice, spam machines at once, or keep betting after the item window ends.

items.label: Simple But Easy To Overplay
items.floorClues: Duck Race appears in Floor 1 and Floor 4 achievement clues. Wheel games appear across Floor 1, Floor 2, Floor 3, and Floor 4 achievement clues.
items.bestFor: Quick decisions, achievement targets, and emergency multiplier attempts.
items.strategy: Set a hard attempt limit before betting. Duck Race is easy to understand, but repeated quick bets can drain the shared bank. Wheel games should be used for one agreed swing, not endless recovery spins.
items.avoidWhen: Avoid this when quota is already safe, players are tilted, or the group keeps saying one more after every miss.

items.label: Later-Floor Testing Games
items.floorClues: These games are tied to Floor 2+ achievement goals, with several appearing again on Floor 3 or Floor 4.
items.bestFor: Teams that understand cash-out timing, multiplier targets, or table fail conditions.
items.strategy: Start with minimum bets. Learn what counts as a win, how to cash out, and where the table can erase money before using serious bets.
items.avoidWhen: Avoid this when the player is still learning the buttons or the team needs stable quota progress.

items.label: Late-Run Specialist Tables
items.floorClues: Dragon Tower, 1P Poker, and Mine Sweeper are Floor 3 and Floor 4 specialist achievement targets.
items.bestFor: Focused achievement routing, item-supported pushes, and experienced groups.
items.strategy: Treat these as planned late-floor targets. Assign one player to learn the rules, protect the attempt with items when possible, and never use the biggest available bet on first contact.
items.avoidWhen: Avoid this when the group has not cleared the early-floor basics or is entering the table only because it looks new.

lucidBlocksBestEarlyUnlocks.title: Gamble With Your Friends Items And Tickets
lucidBlocksBestEarlyUnlocks.intro: Tickets are not only for cosmetics. They decide what your team can do before the next 5-minute casino day starts.

items.title: Golden Chip
items.useCase: Gives one single free all-in, making it one of the cleanest early power buys.
items.bestTiming: Use after choosing the target table and checking whether Taser can raise the max bet first.
items.avoidWhen: Do not fire it on a random table or before the team agrees on the stop point.

items.title: Taser
items.useCase: Use it on a keypad to increase the maximum bet before a larger push.
items.bestTiming: Use before Golden Chip, protected bets, or a profit-buffed table play.
items.avoidWhen: Do not use it before choosing the table; a boosted wrong keypad wastes the combo.

items.title: Holy Statue
items.useCase: Prevents nearby losses while active, which makes it strong for grouped fast-repeat betting.
items.bestTiming: Group players near slots, roulette, wheel games, or another fast table before activation.
items.avoidWhen: Do not keep betting after the protection window ends.

items.title: Drink
items.useCase: Increases profit while drunk, but the player still needs enough control to play the table.
items.bestTiming: Give it to the player who already understands the table and is making the planned push.
items.avoidWhen: Do not give it to the most chaotic player or use it before a table is chosen.

items.title: Microphone
items.useCase: Increases nearby profit while singing, making it better when players are grouped together.
items.bestTiming: Use around a planned group bet, Golden Chip play, or clustered fast-table push.
items.avoidWhen: Do not activate while the team is scattered across different games.

items.title: Insurance
items.useCase: Reduces the pain of a failed bet and pairs well with risky but controlled pushes.
items.bestTiming: Use when the team must keep gambling and a failed attempt would damage the shared bank badly.
items.avoidWhen: Do not treat Insurance as permission to blind max bet a table the player does not understand.

items.title: Quota Gun
items.useCase: Pays 33% of quota for each body part it shoots off, turning body parts into immediate quota value.
items.bestTiming: Use when the timer is low, the team is short on quota, and normal gambling is too slow.
items.avoidWhen: Do not target the player carrying a key item or a player needed for the next day.

items.title: Bonus Draw
items.useCase: Gets ticket value from profit, making it more useful after the group can produce stable wins.
items.bestTiming: Use on longer-term runs where the team can repeatedly profit without risking the whole bank.
items.avoidWhen: Do not buy before basic survival items if the team is still failing quota.

items.title: Angel's Reel
items.useCase: Spins for a chance to revert the last loss.
items.bestTiming: Use after a meaningful failed bet or a loss that affects quota progress.
items.avoidWhen: Do not waste it after a tiny minimum bet.

items.title: Devil's Reel
items.useCase: Can triple or lose the last win.
items.bestTiming: Use only when the last win is large enough to matter and the team accepts the risk.
items.avoidWhen: Do not use after a lucky result that already makes quota safe.

items.title: Mystery Box
items.useCase: Gives a random item and can lead into high-value tools such as Time Machine.
items.bestTiming: Buy after the team already covered the main survival item for the next casino day.
items.avoidWhen: Do not buy when the team needs a specific item to save the next run.

items.title: Time Machine
items.useCase: Time Rewind can rewind up to 60 seconds, giving the team a second chance after a major mistake.
items.bestTiming: Save it for a failed all-in, bad cash-out, bad wheel result, or late-floor mistake.
items.avoidWhen: Do not use it after a tiny loss or repeat the same failed decision after rewinding.

lucidBlocksAchievementTracker.title: Gamble With Your Friends Achievements Guide
lucidBlocksAchievementTracker.intro: Use the achievement list as a floor map. Start with Floor 1 basics, then move into item-supported later floors and money milestones.

items.whatToDo: Learn Roulette, Duck Race, Wheel of Fortune, Street Craps, Blackjack, and Slot Machine with controlled bets before pushing higher floors.
items.routeTip: Good first targets include Lucky 7, YARLL!!!, All on Red, No Bust Run, Jackpot Hunter, High Roller, Big Spender, and Lucky Streak.

items.whatToDo: Work on Penguin Cross, Keno, Crash, HiLo, Plinko, and Money Wheel targets after the team understands Tickets and item support.
items.routeTip: Bring items before chasing high-variance goals such as Greed Test, Spin Streak, and Clean Fall.

items.whatToDo: Treat Floor 3 as a specialist floor with Street Craps, Penguin Cross, Dragon Tower, 1P Poker, Mine Sweeper, HiLo, and Wheel goals.
items.routeTip: Pick one target per day. Natural Roller needs Street Craps point-phase play, while Perfect Sweep and Poker Face should be saved for focused late-run attempts.

items.whatToDo: Use Floor 4 for high-risk table mastery, streaks, big-bet achievements, and deliberate loss-chain achievements.
items.routeTip: Separate serious quota runs from deliberate loss goals such as High Risk Wedge and Straight Down.

items.whatToDo: Collect $10M, $100M, $1B, $10B, and $100B across the money milestone ladder.
items.routeTip: Do not grind the biggest milestones early. They become more realistic once the group understands high floors, item combos, and when to stop.

items.whatToDo: Handle special achievements outside the normal floor-by-floor route.
items.routeTip: Keep awareness-style and special-condition achievements separate from serious quota days.

items.whatToDo: Route the three ending achievements after the team understands quota, Tickets, items, and floor progression.
items.routeTip: Do ending runs after you can consistently survive days without wasting the shared bank.

lucidBlocksSingleplayerAndPlatformFAQ.title: Gamble With Your Friends Endings Guide
lucidBlocksSingleplayerAndPlatformFAQ.intro: Gamble With Your Friends has three endings tied to late-run choices and hidden Steam achievements. Treat ending runs as their own route, not as something to chase during your first quota days.

items.title: Ending Route Overview
items.summary: The Steam page lists around 2-3 hours of playtime with 3 endings.
content: Before pushing endings, learn the daily 5-minute quota loop, the shared bank, item pickup flow, body-part economy, and the difference between safe quota money and extra gambling money.
A clean ending attempt starts in the lobby: assign a banker, choose one main table plan, carry only useful items, and avoid mixing ending progress with random achievement hunting.
Use ending runs after stable Floor 2+ progress, not during the first few learning days.

items.title: Let It Ride!
items.summary: Hidden ending achievement theme: risk it all and become filthy rich again.
content: Achievement description: Like a true gambler, you risked it all and became filthy rich again.
Best route style: wealth-focused late run with strong item planning, high-value table pushes, and a clear decision to keep riding after the team already has major money.
Good prep: Taser + Golden Chip for a planned all-in, profit tools for a table the team understands, and Time Machine reserved for a major failed decision.

items.title: End of the Line
items.summary: Hidden ending achievement theme: reaching the hard stop of the gambling loop.
content: Achievement description: You will not learn, will you?

Best route style: separate from clean money milestone runs. Keep it as a dedicated ending attempt so the team is not trying to protect achievements, quota streaks, and ending progress at the same time.
Good prep: understand late-floor risk tables, keep the shared bank readable, and avoid wasting items before the route-defining decision point.

items.title: ...And They Lived Happily After
items.summary: Hidden ending achievement theme: quitting gambling and starting a new life.
content: Achievement description: Quit gambling and start a new life, congrats.
Best route style: stable late run focused on survival, restraint, and walking away instead of turning every safe day into another risky push.
Good prep: hit quota cleanly, protect the shared bank, avoid unnecessary body-part trades, and stop gambling once the run has enough momentum.

items.title: Ending Run Checklist
items.summary: Use a separate ending plan so the team does not accidentally burn the run.
content: Pick one ending target before entering the casino day.
Choose one main bettor and one shot-caller for risky tables.
Use setup items before payoff items: Taser before Golden Chip, grouping before Holy Statue, and table choice before profit buffs.
Separate deliberate loss-chain achievements from serious ending runs.
Review the money graph and ticket state before deciding whether the next day is a push day or a safe quota day.

lucidBlocksSteamDeckAndController.title: Gamble With Your Friends System Requirements
lucidBlocksSteamDeckAndController.intro: Use these PC requirement checkpoints before launch so your group avoids avoidable setup delays.

faqs.question: What operating system is required?
faqs.answer: Windows 10 or newer is listed for current Steam play sessions.

faqs.question: What processor is listed for minimum setup?
faqs.answer: Core i5-6600 class CPUs are listed for baseline performance.

faqs.question: How much memory is recommended?
faqs.answer: 8 GB RAM is the standard minimum target for stable gameplay sessions.

faqs.question: What graphics baseline should players expect?
faqs.answer: A GTX 1050-level GPU is commonly referenced for the minimum graphics tier.

faqs.question: How much storage should we reserve?
faqs.answer: Keep at least 5 GB free for game files, updates, and local cache growth.

lucidBlocksCrashFixAndTroubleshooting.title: Gamble With Your Friends Troubleshooting and Updates
lucidBlocksCrashFixAndTroubleshooting.intro: When sessions fail, fix order matters. Run this short checklist before escalating issues to community support channels.

steps.title: Verify Game Update State
steps.description: Apply the latest Steam patch first and restart the client before deeper troubleshooting.

steps.title: Confirm Host and Lobby Settings
steps.description: Re-check lobby privacy and host stability, then re-host if players fail to join consistently.

steps.title: Validate Network Consistency
steps.description: Avoid unstable connection routing and keep all party members on predictable network conditions.

steps.title: Collect Error Context
steps.description: Capture clear repro steps, timestamp, and device details so support replies can target the real failure path.

steps.title: Escalate Through Official Channels
steps.description: Submit the issue in official community spaces with logs and reproducible steps for faster triage.

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about this co-op casino crawler.

questions.question: Is Gamble With Your Friends multiplayer only?
questions.answer: No. Solo is available, but the core experience is designed around 1-6 player online co-op coordination.

questions.question: How does the shared bankroll system work?
questions.answer: All players spend from one account, so every large bet affects the entire run and requires team-level risk control.

questions.question: What is the daily quota loop?
questions.answer: Each day gives a short timer to clear quota and keep the run alive, which drives the pacing of every table decision.

questions.question: How many endings and achievements are available?
questions.answer: Current launch content includes three endings and 55 Steam achievements.

questions.question: Where should our team start when learning tables?
questions.answer: Start with lower-volatility tables, lock communication roles, and add high-risk options only when quota pacing is stable.

questions.question: Where can we track updates and community help?
questions.answer: Use the Steam Community Hub and the official Discord for patch notes, troubleshooting, and group coordination.
